so the surgeon is detaching completely the airway.
As the defect was quite wide, the sleeve was the safest option.

This is not recommended by some surgeons because there is no feedback on the robotic instruments, there is no tactile feedback and
A too high traction on the thread could rupture it or cause a rupture of the thread which is damaged in the postoperative period.
Of course, a rupture in any of the points of a continuous suture implies the complete fail of the whole anastomosis.

but the feedback is a visual feedback so even if the surgeon does not have a tactile feedback it can have an idea of how much is pulling on the threads looking at the structure is pulling on and it can prevent stress on the material which can cause damage or even rupture

Carcinoid tumors, especially typical carcinoid tumors, are a slow-growing mass which rarely metastasize and they arise from the airways more often.
So when possible, it is generally accepted to spare the lung parenchyma.

and the procedure is still needed because these tumors tend to grow and to obstruct the distal airways causing atelectasis of the lung parenchyma and also if the carcinoid is atypical it can spread faster and cause metastasis, more aggressive biological behavior.
